meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve no testing knowledge (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no testing knowledge. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: A Student's Guide to Mastering Testing
content.seo_description: Bridge the testing gap. Learn to use AI to debug your code, master Antigravity and Claude Code, and build production-ready software as a student developer.
content.answer_block: Overcome the lack of testing knowledge by leveraging AI as an automated pair programmer. Use Claude Code to generate unit tests based on your logic, then utilize Antigravity to simulate edge cases. This workflow turns debugging from a frustrating trial-and-error process into a structured, pedagogical engineering exercise.
content.intro:
The screen flickers. You’ve just pushed your first full-stack project, and the console is bleeding red. You stare at the stack trace, feeling that familiar, sinking realization: you don’t have a testing strategy. You are a builder, not a QA engineer, and the abyss between 'it works on my machine' and 'production-ready' feels like an insurmountable chasm. 

But the rules of software development have shifted. You no longer need to manually write every assertion or spend hours hunting for typos. By treating AI as a senior mentor, you can bridge the gap between amateur code and professional-grade stability. This is your roadmap to transforming from a student who guesses into an engineer who verifies.

steps.title: Analyze the Failure State
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the failure. Use AI to parse logs and isolate the exact point of execution failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this stack trace and output a JSON representation of the failing module, the expected state versus actual state, and the root cause hypothesis.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read logs/error.log --task 'Identify the root cause of this failure'

steps.title: Generate Baseline Unit Tests
steps.description: Create a safety net. Instruct the AI to write tests for the specific function that is currently breaking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest unit tests for this function focusing on edge cases like null values, empty strings, and boundary integer conditions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create unit tests for src/utils/processor.ts based on the error logic'

steps.title: Execute and Observe
steps.description: Run your new test suite to confirm the failure is reproducible in a controlled environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the newly created tests and provide a summary of the failure modes. Do not suggest a fix yet.

steps.title: Apply AI-Driven Refactoring
steps.description: Now that you have a failing test, let the AI suggest the fix to pass the test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the code to satisfy the failing test cases while maintaining existing functionality. Explain the logic change.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/utils/processor.ts --apply-fix 'Pass the unit tests generated in step 2'

steps.title: Regression and Validation
steps.description: Ensure your fix didn't break other parts of the application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the full test suite and confirm 100% pass rate. If any new warnings appear, suggest a minor refactor.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'check for regressions and verify all tests pass'

pro_tips: Always require the AI to explain the 'Why' behind a fix; if it can't explain it, don't ship it.
Use test-driven development (TDD) by asking the AI to write the test before you write the feature code.
Treat your test folder as a living document; have the AI update tests every time you add a new feature.
Use Claude Code to document your API contracts automatically based on your successful test assertions.
When facing complex bugs, ask the AI to generate a 'Rubber Duck' summary of the code flow to spot logic gaps.
content.next_steps: Once your tests are passing, commit your changes, push to GitHub, and integrate a CI/CD pipeline like GitHub Actions to run these tests automatically on every pull request.
content.conversion_block: You now have the power to turn chaos into code. Don't let a lack of testing experience stop you from shipping your dream app. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
